questions.stem: Walk me through how users authenticate to this system, from the credential they present to how you make sure they are authorized for what they are doing.
cues: Is CAC/PIV the primary authentication credential; exceptions?
For mission partners or non-DoD users, what is the path (PIV-I, IdAM federation)?
Have you eliminated password-only fallbacks?
How is identity governed (ICAM, Active Directory, unified IdP)?
What is your MFA story beyond the smart card factor?
Are sign-in decisions context-aware (location, device, behavior)?

questions.stem: Tell me about how privileged access is managed for this system, especially the controls that distinguish Target-phase from Advanced-phase capability.
cues: Do you use a PAM tool with vaulted check-out, session recording, and just-in-time elevation?
Are admin sessions monitored with UEBA?
How quickly is a compromised privileged account detected and contained?
Are non-person privileged identities governed the same as people?
How do you handle break-glass / emergency credentials?
What is the audit trail for every privileged action?

questions.stem: Walk me through how you know what devices touch this system and how you decide which ones are trusted enough to allow access.
cues: Where is your authoritative device inventory; how are non-person entities tracked?
Are devices fingerprinted on every authentication?
Do you have continuous compliance checking (DISA STIG, encryption, patch level)?
How do you handle devices that do not meet baseline (block, quarantine, limited access)?
What about IoT, OT, and embedded devices in mission contexts?
Is your CMDB integrated with your access policies?

questions.stem: Tell me how continuously you re-evaluate device health and how the device's signal feeds into access decisions for this system.
cues: Is device posture re-checked at every session, every hour, continuously?
Does a posture change automatically revoke access?
How does endpoint EDR/XDR coverage extend to mobile, BYOD, and cloud workloads?
Are you detecting unusual device behavior in real time?
How do you handle remote and disconnected operations?

questions.stem: Walk me through how this system's network is segmented and how traffic flowing across it is protected.
cues: Is the network segmented at the perimeter, internally, or both?
Have you moved toward micro-segmentation or software-defined networking?
Is east-west traffic encrypted (server-to-server, container-to-container)?
What about traffic in and out of cloud and mission-partner networks?
Do you inspect TLS-encrypted traffic; how do you handle the visibility-vs-privacy tradeoff?
For classified environments, what is the segregation between security domains?

questions.stem: Tell me how you maintain visibility across this network and how the system holds up under stress.
cues: What is your network telemetry coverage (on-prem, cloud, mission partner)?
How quickly can you detect lateral movement or anomalous east-west traffic?
Do you have DDoS protection and redundant paths?
For hybrid and multi-cloud, how do you correlate network signals across providers?
What is your network's resilience in degraded or contested environments?

questions.stem: Walk me through how applications are protected for this system, both how access is granted and how the applications themselves are secured.
cues: Where is your application inventory maintained; includes SaaS?
Is application access controlled centrally (SSO, IdAM-driven) or per-app?
What is your story for API security (authentication, rate limiting, vulnerability testing)?
For container and Kubernetes workloads, what runtime protections are in place?
How are application threats detected and blocked?

questions.stem: Tell me about the path applications take from development through deployment, and how you are catching issues at each step.
cues: Is your SDLC documented and followed with security gates?
What runs in CI (SAST, DAST, SCA, secrets scanning)?
How are container images vetted (signed, scanned, attested)?
Is SBOM tracked for every workload?
What runtime monitoring is in place (RASP, eBPF observability)?
How do you handle third-party and supply-chain risk in software?

questions.stem: Give me the picture of how data is tagged, where it lives, and how its sensitivity drives the controls around it.
cues: Is data tagged at creation (author, automated discovery, or both)?
What classification scheme (Public / CUI / Confidential / Secret / Top Secret as applicable)?
Are tags persistent across storage systems?
Where does sensitive data reside (on-prem, FedRAMP cloud, mission partner)?
How is encryption applied at rest, in transit, in use?
Who manages encryption keys; are they rotated automatically?

questions.stem: Tell me how you protect data once it has been classified, both who can access it and how you prevent it from going where it should not.
cues: How are data access decisions made (role-based, attribute-based, mission-context-based)?
Do you use Data Rights Management that follows data outside the original system?
Where is DLP applied (endpoint, network, cloud APIs, email)?
How do you monitor anomalous data access (large downloads, off-hours, unfamiliar locations)?
What is your data egress story (approved destinations, blocked exfiltration)?
For mission-partner sharing, how is need-to-know enforced?

questions.stem: Walk me through what you collect, how it gets to your analysts, and how you turn signals into action.
cues: What telemetry sources feed your SIEM/XDR (endpoints, network, identity, apps, cloud)?
What is your integration with JFHQ-DODIN, USCYBERCOM, or other shared sensing?
How long is data retained; sufficient for incident response and forensics?
Are threat intelligence feeds integrated and operationalized?
What is your detection development lifecycle (building, tuning, retiring)?
How are AI/ML models used for anomaly detection; monitored for drift?

questions.stem: Tell me how automation supports security operations for this system, what is automated today, what is still manual, and where you are heading.
cues: Is there a SOAR platform; what playbooks are fully automated end-to-end?
Can your security stack automatically respond (isolate device, disable user, block IP)?
How integrated are your tools (shared signals or silos)?
Are AI/ML models used for detection, response decisions, or both?
How do you monitor the automation itself for correct behavior?
For DoD operational tempo, what role does automation play in incident response time?
